problem: Generate deterministic Python code that transforms YAML source files (bodygraph.yaml, channels.yaml, centers.yaml) into HD_ONTOLOGY_complete.json with zero interpretation or transcription errors
goal: Produce Python script ontology/generate_ontology.py that reads YAML sources and outputs HD_ONTOLOGY_complete.json through deterministic transformation - ensuring 100% accuracy by eliminating human/LLM transcription errors

current_issue.problem: Strand d380b69d generated HD_ONTOLOGY_complete.json with transcription errors (11+ gate complements wrong, other inaccuracies)
current_issue.root_cause: LLM interpretation introduced errors when copying data from YAML to JSON
current_issue.solution: Write deterministic code that mechanically transforms YAML → JSON with zero interpretation

architectural_principle.core_idea: YAML files are source of truth - ontology is a derived artifact
architectural_principle.pattern: Code-generated data > Hand-written data > LLM-interpreted data
architectural_principle.precedent: Similar to how RawBodyGraph is calculated deterministically from Swiss Ephemeris

source_files.validated_by_user: User hand-audited these files - treat as 100% correct
files: src/human_design/bodygraph.yaml - 64 gates with I Ching names, complements, zodiac ranges, Ra descriptions
src/human_design/channels.yaml - 36 channels as gate pairs with names
source_files.principle: NEVER override or 'correct' source data - if YAML says gate 16 complement is 48, ontology MUST say 48

not_in_yaml: Type definitions (Initiator/Builder/Specialist/Coordinator/Observer) - from HD system knowledge
Authority hierarchy - from HD system knowledge
Profile descriptions (12 profiles) - from HD system knowledge
Circuit classifications - from gates_data.py or HD system knowledge
Quarter themes - from HD system knowledge
Center descriptions (defined/undefined) - from HD system knowledge
additional_data.approach: Hardcode HD system knowledge in Python as constants/dictionaries, but YAML data must be parsed not interpreted

constraints: Work in strand fork space (automatic isolation branch)
Write DETERMINISTIC code - same input YAML must always produce identical output JSON
NEVER interpret or 'improve' YAML data - copy exactly
Code must be runnable: python ontology/generate_ontology.py → produces ontology/HD_ONTOLOGY_complete.json
Include validation: assert generated data matches expected counts (64 gates, 36 channels, 9 centers)
Use Python standard library only (no external dependencies beyond PyYAML if needed)
Code must be self-documenting with clear structure
success_criteria: All 64 gate complements match bodygraph.yaml EXACTLY
All 36 channel gate pairs match channels.yaml EXACTLY
All 9 center gate lists match centers.yaml EXACTLY
Gate numbers, I Ching names, zodiac ranges copied exactly from bodygraph.yaml
64keys terminology used consistently (Initiator not Manifestor, LIFEFORCE not Sacral)
Traditional HD terms in traditional_name fields
Script is idempotent: running twice produces identical output
Validation assertions in code verify data integrity
README.md or docstring explains how to regenerate ontology

tasks.task: Design Python script architecture for deterministic YAML→JSON transformation: (1) Define data structures for parsed YAML, (2) Design transformation functions (parse_gates, parse_channels, parse_centers), (3) Design HD system knowledge constants (types, authorities, profiles), (4) Design JSON output structure, (5) Design validation assertions. Emphasize zero-interpretation principle.

Implement ontology/generate_ontology.py: (1) Parse bodygraph.yaml (gates with complements, I Ching names, zodiac), (2) Parse channels.yaml (36 gate pairs), (3) Parse centers.yaml (9 centers with gates), (4) Hardcode HD system data (types, authorities, profiles, circuits, quarters), (5) Transform to JSON structure, (6) Validate output, (7) Write ontology/HD_ONTOLOGY_complete.json. Include docstring explaining usage.

3_validation_synthesis.description: Validate generated ontology and document approach

tasks.task: Synthesize codegen approach and validate output: (1) Run generate_ontology.py and verify output, (2) Cross-check generated ontology against source YAML files, (3) Verify 64 gates, 36 channels, 9 centers all match exactly, (4) Document transformation logic, (5) Provide usage instructions for future regeneration.

determinism: Running script twice produces byte-identical output
No randomness, no LLM calls, no interpretation
Same YAML input always produces same JSON output
accuracy: All channel gate pairs match channels.yaml
All center gate lists match centers.yaml
I Ching names copied exactly from bodygraph.yaml

metadata.isolation: automatic git fork and branch creation
metadata.principle: Code-generated data is more reliable than LLM-interpreted data
